rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/13/21. Despite the executor's opinion that Probate Code Section 12200 does not have "set requirements, other than to 'show the condition of the estate,'" etc., Probate Code Section 12201(b) requires Notice of hearing of the report to be given as provided in Section 1220, for which notice the Judicial Council has adopted mandatory form DE-120. No proof of service of proper notice has been filed. Courtesy copies of the proof of service and any other papers filed after 8/19/20 must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/4/21. (It is also noted that the petition verified on 8/18/20 sought an additional 90 days, which time has already elapsed, but no petition for final distribution has been filed.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CYNTHIA MAUREEN BELLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/13/21. There is no Exhibit B attached to the Second Declaration filed on 12/2/20. Merely attaching an unfiled creditor's claim to a declaration is not the same as filing it timely. And no Allowance or Rejection was submitted to the Court to allow or reject the claim. The attachment to the claim still does not show who the payees were. Exhibit C to the Second Declaration is merely a printout of billing information; there is no indication of what was filed or processed, etc., for the Court to determine if such cost is a reimbursable one. If both heirs consented to the charitable gifts purportedly requested by the decedent before she died, the father's consent must be filed. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner before 1/4/21.

rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO PLACER COUNTY** CONSERVATORSHIP OF PETER J. BUXTUN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/27/2021 for a court investigator's report to be completed and reviewed. 1) The beginning date of this account selected by the conservator of the estate is 04/08/2019, the date of issuance of letters, but the date of appointment is 04/04/2019. 2) Paragraph 3 of the petition does not state the amount of the supplemental inventory and appraisal No. 1 (sentence appears incomplete). 3) Has the conservator of the estate given copies of the partial and final inventories and appraisals to the person who requested special notice? Probate Code section 2702(b). 4) Paragraph 3 and the status report allege that the conservatee moved to Placer County in June 2020, but according to the pre-move notice (filed on 08/05/2020), the move date was 08/15/2020. No post-move notice has been filed. 5) No fee schedule or statement of compensation has been filed by either petitioner. Probate Code sections 1821(c)(1) & 2614.7. 6) No determination of level of care form signed by both conservators of the person has been filed. Probate Code sections 2352.5(b)(c) & 2105(c). 7) The amount of compensation of the co-conservator of the person alleged in paragraph 7 of the petition and paragraph 5 of the prayer for relief is different from the amount shown on Exhibit B. 8) The amount of the initial bond alleged in paragraph 8 appears incorrect. 9) Paragraph 9 alleges that the conservatorship estate does not include any money received from the VA, but according to the petition (filed on 07/25/2019), the conservatee is entitled to VA benefits. 10) The ending year of the account on the summary of account appears incorrect. 11) No original bank statements verifying the cash balances as of 04/03/2019 & 04/30/2020 have been filed. 12) Please provide copies of stock certificates or transaction statements from transfer agents showing the reported securities. 13) According to the court investigator's report (filed on 03/29/2019), the conservatee receives gold dividends, but Schedule A does not show any. 14) Schedule C shows expenses related to Acura, but this car has been inventoried and appraised. 15) Schedule C shows payments of $12,261 + $435 to the court-appointed attorney on 01/23/2020, but the amount of compensation ordered by the Court is $12,696 plus costs of $435. 16) Please identify all the tangible personal items (by item number) held in the storage. 17) Re Exhibit A, the service on 09/23/2019 appears to have been rendered for the estate, rather than for the person.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PORTSIDE 503 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Petitioner, Francis X. Browne, To Compel Trustee To Post Bond
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. The Motion of Petitioner, Francis X. Browne, to Compel Trustee to Post Bond is granted, pursuant to Probate Code section 15602(a)(2). Notwithstanding that the current existence of the Portside 503 Trust is disputed and will not be determined on this motion, Petitioner has alleged in a verified pleading that he has a present interest as a beneficiary of the Portside 503 Trust to a distribution of decedent's 2010 Bentley Speed upon his death, as well as other articles of decedent's personal property. Respondent has not disputed in the motion that the Portside 503 Trust existed at some point in time, or that Petitioner was entitled to such a distribution upon decedent's death. Petitioner has claimed a non-contingent beneficiary interest which necessitates protection pursuant to Probate Code section 15602(a)(2). The parties did not dispute in the papers that the specific vehicle listed in the 2015 draft Trust document became unavailable in 2019 and an insurance claim was paid out in the amount of $80,095.56. For the limited purposes of this motion, the court will adopt this valuation as the minimum value of Petitioner's non-contingent beneficial interest in the Portside 503 Trust. Petitioner has not offered, and the court has not found, authority under which he entitled to a bond in an amount equal to the penalty he seeks under Probate Code section 859. As discussed in Estate of Young (2008) 160 Cal. App. 4th 62, 89 and Estate of Ashlock (2020) 45 Cal. App. 5th 1066, 1077, before determination of liability under section 859, the court must find there has been a wrongful taking. The court will not determine that in this motion. Respondent is ordered to post bond of $81,000, plus costs of bond. The cost of the bond shall be charged against the trust. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on December 15, 2020. Remote appearance is required, whenever possible, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. The appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/10/21. Petition may be taken off calendar at the next hearing if the following are not filed at least two weeks prior to the next hearing: 1) Duties and Liabilities; 2) Notice of Petition to Administer Estate showing that notice was provided at least 15 days prior to the hearing date shown on the notice; 3) Supplemental Declaration providing the information that was not provided in Item 2 and 3 of the petition.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES JENNINGS IV
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/10/21 for continued failure to address Examiner's Notes. Petition may be taken off calendar at the next hearing if Examiner's Notes is not responded to at least 2 weeks prior.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM KOONG LEE HO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/19/2021. 1) Since the petitioner is also the PR of the estate of the sole heir, have to notice the heirs and beneficiaries of the estate of the heir, Jenny Ho (14.26.E.2). File a declaration identifying those heirs and beneficiaries and file POS for them. 2) Petitioner requests $170,500.00 bond. The bond amount will be rounded up to the nearest thousand dollars. [LRSF 14.44] 3) A hard copy of the proposed order must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court no later than 4pm on 01/04/2021. (LRSF 14.93). [Proposed orders may not be e-filed.]
